cellestial.frames
=================

.. py:module:: cellestial.frames


Submodules
----------

.. toctree::
   :maxdepth: 1

   /autoapi/cellestial/frames/build/index


Functions
---------

.. autoapisummary::

   cellestial.frames.anndata_observations_frame
   cellestial.frames.anndata_variables_frame
   cellestial.frames.build_frame


Package Contents
----------------

.. py:function:: anndata_observations_frame(data: anndata.AnnData, /, variable_keys: str | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, *, observations_name='barcode', include_dimensions: bool | int = False) -> polars.DataFrame

   Build an Observations DataFrame from an AnnData object.

   :param data: The AnnData object containing the observations.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param variable_keys: Variable keys to add to the DataFrame. If None, no additional keys are added.
   :type variable_keys: str | Sequence[str] | None
   :param observations_name: The name of the observations column, default is "barcode".
   :type observations_name: str, optional
   :param include_dimensions: Whether to include dimensions from `obsm` in the DataFrame, default is False.
                              Providing an integer will limit the number of dimensions to given number.
   :type include_dimensions: bool | int

   :returns: A DataFrame containing the observations, with optional variable keys and dimensions.
   :rtype: DataFrame


.. py:function:: anndata_variables_frame(data: anndata.AnnData, *, variables_name: str = 'variable', include_dimensions: bool | int = False) -> polars.DataFrame

   Build a Variables DataFrame from an AnnData object.

   :param data: The AnnData object containing the variables.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param variables_name: Name for the variables index column, default is 'variable'
   :type variables_name: str
   :param include_dimensions: Whether to include dimensions from `varm` in the DataFrame, default is False.
                              Providing an integer will limit the number of dimensions to given number.
   :type include_dimensions: bool | int

   :returns: A DataFrame containing the variables.
   :rtype: DataFrame


.. py:function:: build_frame(data: anndata.AnnData, *, variable_keys: str | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, axis: Literal[0, 1] | None = None, observations_name: str = 'barcode', variables_name: str = 'variable', include_dimensions: bool | int = False) -> polars.DataFrame

   Build a `polars.DataFrame` from an AnnData object.

   :param data: The AnnData object containing the variables.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param variable_keys: Variable keys to add to the DataFrame. If None, no additional keys are added.
   :type variable_keys: str | Sequence[str] | None
   :param axis: The axis to build the frame for. 0 for observations, 1 for variables.
   :type axis: Literal[0,1] | None
   :param observations_name: The name of the observations column, default is "barcode".
   :type observations_name: str
   :param variables_name: Name for the variables index column, default is 'variable'
   :type variables_name: str
   :param include_dimensions: Whether to include dimensions in the DataFrame, default is False.
                              Providing an integer will limit the number of dimensions to given number.
   :type include_dimensions: bool | int

   :returns: A polars DataFrame containing the variables.
   :rtype: DataFrame
